raw_comment:
What do you notice?
-Over the years, China had the steepest increase in electricity generation, for all types of power, compared to the United States and India. 
- In 2000, China had lower amounts of electricity generation than the US but surpassed the US by around 2010. 
- The overall electricity generation in the US has not increased by much over the years, but there is a larger proportion of clean fuels being used instead of fossil fuels (especially coal)
- India’s electricity generation remained lower than both China and the US but has steadily increased over the years. 

What do you wonder?
- I wonder if the increase in electricity generation in China is correlated with how “open” their economy was. I know that China has worked on expanding its private sector. 
- I wonder why US electricity generation has plateaued, and if this phenomenon is also seen in other Western developed countries. 
- What is the relationship between energy generation and the political stability of a country?

How does this relate to you and your community?
- This relates to how I might think/vote about certain energy policies in the US. 
- I see a lot more Teslas in the Bay Area - electric cars don’t seem like they are reserved for the rich anymore; many middle-class folks are buying Teslas to account for rising gas prices

Create a catchy headline that captures the graph’s main idea.
Our Energy Usage Has Transformed Over the Last 20 Years
ace_sentences: China had the steepest increase in electricity generation over the years.
This increase was for all types of power.
This increase was compared to the United States and India.
In 2000, China had lower amounts of electricity generation than the US.
China surpassed the US by around 2010.
The overall electricity generation in the US has not increased by much over the years.
There is a larger proportion of clean fuels being used in the US over the years.
These clean fuels are used instead of fossil fuels.
Coal is a fossil fuel.
India's electricity generation remained lower than both China and the US.
India's electricity generation has steadily increased over the years.
I wonder if the increase in electricity generation in China is correlated with how open their economy was.
I know that China has worked on expanding its private sector.
I wonder why US electricity generation has plateaued.
I wonder if this phenomenon is also seen in other Western developed countries.
I wonder what the relationship is between energy generation and the political stability of a country.
This relates to how I might think about certain energy policies in the US.
This relates to how I might vote about certain energy policies in the US.
I see a lot more Teslas in the Bay Area.
Teslas are electric cars.
Electric cars do not seem like they are reserved for the rich anymore.
Many middle-class folks are buying Teslas.
Many middle-class folks are buying Teslas to account for rising gas prices.
The headline is: Our Energy Usage Has Transformed Over the Last 20 Years.
